broll_items.selected_text: The necessity of private networks and subnetting to separate public-facing websites from secure databases and payment systems.
broll_items.title: Private Networks and Subnetting
broll_items.problem: Public-facing websites and secure databases/payment systems are vulnerable to attacks.
broll_items.solution: Private networks and subnetting separate public-facing websites from secure databases and payment systems, enhancing security.
broll_items.reason_for_selection: This segment explains a critical concept that is easier to understand with visual aids, such as diagrams showing the separation of public and private networks.

broll_items.selected_text: Security implementation using Security Groups, NACLs, or NSGs to block vulnerabilities and DOS attacks.
broll_items.title: Security Implementation
broll_items.reason_for_selection: This segment lists different security measures that can be visually represented as a list or a diagram, making it easier for viewers to understand and compare the options.

broll_items.selected_text: Handling remote access and communication between regions or data centers using VPNs and private connections.
broll_items.title: Remote Access and Communication
broll_items.problem: Remote access and communication between regions or data centers are vulnerable to security threats.
broll_items.solution: VPNs and private connections provide secure remote access and communication between regions or data centers.
broll_items.reason_for_selection: This segment presents a problem and solution that can be visually explained using diagrams or animations, making it easier for viewers to understand the concept.
